message.content:
Репозиторий: /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP (монорепо: server/ Node+TS+Express+Mongoose+OpenAI; client/ React+Vite — это админка).
Ревью изменений ветки feat/knowledge-bank поверх базы 6431bf3..HEAD (2 коммита: админ-UI банка + переменный размер теста и качество вопросов).
Сначала прочитай диапазон диффа:  git -C /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP diff 6431bf3 HEAD -- <относящиеся пути>
Затем читай окружающий код, чтобы понять контекст. Ищи РЕАЛЬНЫЕ дефекты корректности и интеграции, а не стиль.
Контекст фич:
 - Бэкенд KC/банк (questionBank.service.ts, ktp.controller.ts, ktp.routes.ts): новый read-эндпоинт listItems вопросов банка (admin); size-параметр assembleNodeTest (разрешено 5/10/15/20), size подмешан в реюз-хэш; generateForCoverage переписан на циклы раундов + разброс difficulty; contentHashOf теперь хеширует текст+варианты+правильный ответ.
 - Test.model.ts: валидатор questions.length сменён с ===10 на диапазон 1..120.
 - test.routes.ts/test.controller.ts: /tests/node-bank принимает size; /generate(+guest) валидируют questionCount.
 - client: ktp.api.ts (+методы KC/банка), ktp.types.ts (+типы), components/ktp/TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx (новый), KtpCatalogPage.tsx (врезка per-topic), гейтинг по role==='admin'.
Возвращай только обоснованные находки с file/location/severity/detail/suggestedFix.

Адверсариально ПРОВЕРЬ находку — попробуй её ОПРОВЕРГНУТЬ, прочитав реальный код. Если это не настоящий дефект (ложноположительное, или уже корректно обрабатывается), верни isReal=false. По умолчанию при сомнении — isReal=false.

Находка:
{
  "file": "server/src/services/questionBank.service.ts",
  "location": "contentHashOf (строки 33-41) + изменение схемы хэша без миграции",
  "severity": "medium",
  "title": "Смена формулы contentHashOf ломает дедуп против уже сохранённых item'ов (v1 хэши)",
  "detail": "contentHashOf переписан с text-only на text+sorted(options)+correctOption и одновременно BANK_PROMPT_VERSION поднят до bank-v2, но миграции уже сохранённых записей нет (grep по contentHash/migrat — только этот сервис и модель, миграций нет). Уникальный индекс {subjectId, knowledgeNodeId, contentHash} теперь не поймает повторную генерацию вопроса, семантически идентичного pre-v2 записи: новый хэш считается по новой формуле и не совпадёт с лежащим в БД старым text-only хэшем. Итог: при дозаполнении банка узлов, заполненных до этой ветки, появятся почти-дубликаты (тот же текст и тот же правильный ответ), которые раньше отсекались. selectCovering их не дедуплицирует — они попадут в тест как разные item'ы.",
  "suggestedFix": "Либо одноразовый бэкфилл contentHash для существующих item'ов по новой формуле (скрипт пересчёта), либо в дедупе сравнивать оба варианта хэша на переходный период. Минимум — задокументировать, что на узлах с pre-v2 банком возможны дубликаты, и прогнать пересчёт перед релизом."
}

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Let me verify the technical claim in the finding more carefully. The finding's core assertion is that the v1 hash and v2 hash differ, so duplicates can be inserted. Let me confirm the exact v1 vs v2 hash formulas and whether the unique index would actually allow a near-duplicate. The key question: does the new hash produce a DIFFERENT value than the old one for the same question, allowing insertion of a semantic duplicate?

The diff confirms:
- v1: `sha256(norm(questionText))` — text only
- v2: `sha256(norm(questionText) | sorted(norm(options)) | =norm(correctOption))` — different input, different hash

So for a question with identical text+options+answer to a pre-v2 stored item, the v2 hash will NOT equal the stored v1 hash, and the unique index `{subjectId, knowledgeNodeId, contentHash}` won't catch it. The technical mechanism is real. Let me verify `selectCovering` doesn't dedupe by text and check how robust the "empty DB" assumption is.

Confirmed: `selectCovering` deduplicates only by `_id` (`usedIds`), not by content text. So if two near-duplicate items (same text/options/answer, different `_id` because v1 hash ≠ v2 hash let both into the DB) both become `active`, they can both be picked into one test as distinct items. The mechanism described in the finding is real.

Now I have enough to assess the finding adversarially. Let me weigh the two key factors:

1. **Technical mechanism — REAL.** v1 hash (`text` only) ≠ v2 hash (`text|sorted(options)|=correctOption`) for the same question. The unique index `{subjectId, knowledgeNodeId, contentHash}` will not catch a re-generated semantic duplicate of a pre-v2 item. `selectCovering` dedups only by `_id`, so two near-duplicates can land in the same test. There is no migration/backfill script in the repo (confirmed: only `seedSubjects`/`seedProfileSubjectPairs`, no migration dir, no `contentHash` recompute).

2. **Real-world impact / preconditions.** The duplicate only manifests when:
   - A node's bank was populated *before* this branch (v1 hashes in DB), AND
   - `generateForCoverage` runs again on that same node and the LLM re-emits a question with identical normalized text+options+answer, AND
   - both copies end up `active` and selected into the same test.

The bank feature itself was introduced only on 2026-06-19 (commit `473e791`), 4 days before this branch, and this is an MVP admin tool. So pre-existing v1 data is plausible but limited. The collision also depends on the LLM regenerating a near-identical question — non-deterministic but realistic given grounded generation on the same source text.

Let me confirm there is genuinely no backfill anywhere (e.g., in package.json scripts or a one-off).

Confirmed: no backfill, recompute, or migration script exists anywhere for `contentHash`.

Assessment: The finding is technically accurate. The hash formula genuinely changed (v1 text-only → v2 text+options+answer), there's genuinely no migration, the unique index genuinely won't catch a regenerated semantic duplicate of a pre-v2 item, and `selectCovering` genuinely dedups only by `_id` so duplicates can co-occur in one test. The data-loss/duplicate path is real for any node whose bank was populated before this branch.

This is a real defect, but its severity is correctly bounded at medium: it requires pre-existing v1 data on a node AND a non-deterministic LLM re-emitting a near-identical question AND both being selected — it degrades test quality (occasional near-dupes) rather than causing crashes or data corruption. The medium rating is appropriate.

input.reason:
Находка подтверждена чтением реального кода.

Механизм реален:
1. contentHashOf переписан: v1 = sha256(norm(questionText)) (text-only), v2 = sha256(norm(text) | sorted(norm(options)) | =norm(correctOption)). Для семантически идентичного вопроса v2-хэш гарантированно НЕ совпадёт с лежащим в БД v1-хэшем — другой вход, другой digest. Файл: server/src/services/questionBank.service.ts, contentHashOf (стр. 31-44), вызов на стр. 239.
2. Уникальный индекс {subjectId, knowledgeNodeId, contentHash} (server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts:89) ловит дубликат только по равенству хэшей. При другом хэше insert проходит (код 11000 не срабатывает), создаётся почти-дубликат.
3. Миграции/бэкфилла нет: BANK_PROMPT_VERSION поднят до bank-v2, но grep по contentHash/backfill/recompute/migrat в server/src/scripts, package.json и по всему репо ничего не дал; каталога миграций нет (есть только seedSubjects/seedProfileSubjectPairs).
4. selectCovering (стр. 350-412) дедуплицирует ТОЛЬКО по _id (usedIds = Set<string> от String(it._id)), а не по тексту. Значит два near-duplicate item'а с разными _id оба могут попасть в один тест.

Адверсариальная проверка — попытка опровергнуть:
- Может, банк-фича новая и v1-данных нет? Нет: фича (с v1 text-only хэшем) введена в 473e791 от 2026-06-19, за 4 дня до этой ветки (база 6431bf3 от 2026-06-22), и прожила несколько коммитов. v1-записи на узлах, заполненных до ветки, реально возможны.
- Может, дедуп всё же по тексту где-то ещё? Нет, selectCovering — единственный отбор, дедуп по _id.
- Может, есть бэкфилл вне scripts? Нет, подтверждено grep'ом по всему серверу.

Severity = medium (а не выше) обоснованно: дефект проявляется только при стечении условий — узел заполнен до ветки (есть v1-хэши) И generateForCoverage добирает вопросы И недетерминированный LLM повторно выдаёт вопрос с идентичным нормализованным текстом+опциями+ответом И обе копии активны и выбраны в один тест. Это деградация качества теста (изредка near-дубли), а не падение/порча данных.

suggestedFix из находки корректен: одноразовый бэкфилл contentHash по новой формуле для существующих item'ов перед релизом, либо на переходный период сравнивать оба варианта хэша при дедупе, либо как минимум задокументировать и прогнать пересчёт на узлах с pre-v2 банком.

content.text:
I have verified the finding by reading the actual code. It is a real defect.

**Verdict: isReal = true, severity = medium**

The finding holds up to adversarial verification:

- **Hash formula genuinely changed** — `questionBank.service.ts:31-44`: v1 was `sha256(norm(questionText))` (text-only), v2 is `sha256(norm(text) | sorted(norm(options)) | =norm(correctOption))`. Different input → different digest for the same question.
- **No migration exists** — confirmed by grep across `server/src/scripts`, `package.json`, and the whole repo for `contentHash`/`backfill`/`recompute`/`migrat`; there is no migrations directory, only `seedSubjects`/`seedProfileSubjectPairs`. `BANK_PROMPT_VERSION` was bumped to `bank-v2` without backfilling stored items.
- **Unique index won't catch it** — `QuestionItem.model.ts:89` keys on `contentHash`; a v2 hash never equals a stored v1 hash, so the duplicate insert succeeds (no 11000).
- **No content-level dedup downstream** — `selectCovering` (`questionBank.service.ts:350-412`) dedups only by `_id` (`usedIds`), not by text, so two near-duplicates can land in the same test.

Attempts to refute it failed: the bank feature (with the v1 hash) was introduced in `473e791` on 2026-06-19, predating this branch's base by 4 days, so pre-v2 data on nodes is plausible; there is no alternate text-based dedup; and there is no backfill anywhere in the server.

Medium severity is appropriate — the bug requires a specific conjunction (pre-v2 data on a node + `generateForCoverage` re-run + the non-deterministic LLM re-emitting a near-identical question + both copies active and selected), and the impact is degraded test quality (occasional near-duplicates), not a crash or data corruption.
